settings.title: A Sentimental Thread: Curating A Wedding Day With Intention
settings.description:
“It feels so good to know two people so in love.” The Zombies sang that in 1968, but anyone who’s been to a truly great wedding also knows the feeling. For a single day, it’s like stepping into a private universe with all your favourite people – looking like you truly belong on cloud nine, dancing to the best music, and moving through moments you’ll remember for a lifetime, with people you hope you’ll be dancing with for the rest of this lifetime. 

The more weddings you go to, the clearer it becomes: the most magical celebrations aren’t the biggest or the most elaborate – they’re the ones where every detail feels like it could only belong to the couple at the centre of it all. As though they were custom made for one another, and the day itself conjured as a reflection of their love.

settings.content_intro:
DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? Is it because it reminds you of a place, someone special or loved ones from generations past? I always like to remind clients that jewellery doesn’t have to be engraved or marked with a special stone to become solely yours – it can be anything about the piece that sparks a feeling or takes you back to a moment in time.

There’s a sense of romance and allure inherit in vintage pieces, and finding the perfect design should feel akin to falling in love: a lightning bolt of recognition; the sense that you two were destined to be connected.

settings.description:
Start With The Story

If you’re planning your own special day and wondering how to make it feel just as personal, start with details that hold meaning. That’s the thinking behind our Language Of Love collection — a curation of mindfully crafted pieces, each carrying its own personal message: a silk thread tying together a piece that tells your story, morse code patterns spelling out vows only you know, and lustrous pearls that feel both timeless and entirely your own.

settings.title:
“Connor and I care so deeply about our community - the people around us have been the welcomed third person in our relationship since the very beginning – from family, to dear friends. We wrote each of our guests a letter to thank them for coming and printed off a fun polaroid of a moment we’ve shared together as our place cards – this was our token to them of how much their presence meant to us on the day. We also did one for each other as a surprise on the day – such special memories I’ll keep forever!”


- Shona, Head Designer.

settings.description:
Let Colour Lead

Carrying that same sense of intention into the rest of your day is about thoughtful layering. Let colour guide you — soft ivory for enduring love, a whisper of blue for trust, deep red for passion and protection. These can appear anywhere: tied into your bouquet, woven through your table settings, or printed on your invitations.


Layer In Your History

Bring in touchstones from your shared history: a dessert from your first date, a poem slipped under each guest’s plate, music from the moment you first met. These gestures – some barely visible, others centre-stage – are what weave your story through the celebration.

settings.description:
“We chose the iconic Rose Seidler House as the backdrop to our day, a place that speaks to our shared love of design and mid-century architecture. Colour became the thread that tied it all together - playful, modern yet nostalgic - a reflection of the fun, childlike joy we wanted our wedding to hold. It felt true to us and our relationship: vibrant, full of character, and rooted in the things we love most.”

- Madeleine, Social Media Coordinator.

settings.description:
“Music has always been the language of our relationship, and certain songs have become markers of the moments, people, and places that shaped us. On our wedding day we carefully curated songs for milestone moments that carried those memories. Melodies that reminded us of family, or moments along our journey, and songs that honoured the people who built the path to our life together. What is beautiful is that those songs have stayed with us, quietly threading through our everyday moments. Now, as our children grow up, those same tunes surface in our home and travels, embedding themselves into their memories too, so the soundtrack of our love story keeps unfolding.”

- Natalie, Founder.

settings.description:
Ask The Only Question That Matters

Keep asking: “Does this feel like us?” If it does, you’re building a day that will live in memory as vividly as the vows themselves – a day where, for everyone there, it feels so good to know two people so in love.

settings.title: The Language Of Love
settings.description: At the heart of our new six-piece collection is a hidden script: morse code messages carrying vows in gold and freshwater pearls. Whether chosen for a wedding day or simply as a vow for a lifetime, organic silhouettes and woven silk threads in three tones complete each piece, designed to hold a message that’s yours alone.
